title: While the West lights fires, China walks through the smoke
body: Al Jazeera reports the Houthis are waving Chinese ships through while Western and Saudi tankers burn or stay home, crossings just hit a two-month low. The US is trading strikes for infrastructure and watching oil blow past $100.

It isn't only shipping. OCCRP mapped how China's central bank has quietly bought into Europe's critical infrastructure, a police headquarters, wind farms, natural gas, routed through shell companies in the British Virgin Islands. And Rest of World finds that even as Washington tries to contain Chinese AI, Apple and Silicon Valley keep reaching for Chinese models like Kimi K3, while China recruits its next generation of engineers straight out of high school.
